title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: An academic liveblog and summary of Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town. The coverage details his co-authored research analyzing how partisan alignment, news quality, and Facebook's shifting algorithmic governance affect how widely shared news URLs reach their audiences.
description: An academic liveblog post summarizing Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the 2026 ICA conference regarding how Facebook's algorithmic shifts impact engagement with news URLs over time.

title: Disinfo Update: Civil Society Mobilises | Council of Europe Actions | MFF Budgets
snippet: In its June 2026 newsletter, EU DisinfoLab features Fabio Giglietto’s co-authored paper, 'Beyond the share button: How partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic governance shape what millions see on Facebook,' highlighting its key findings on platform transparency and content amplification.
description: An EU DisinfoLab newsletter featuring Fabio Giglietto's co-authored research on how partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic governance influence content visibility on Facebook.

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: A press report highlighting the impact of Fabio Giglietto's computational social science study on Meta's algorithms. The coverage outlines how his research inspired a segment on Rai3's investigative program 'Report' and triggered a formal inquiry within the European Parliament.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: An investigative piece from Open covering a study by Fabio Giglietto that examined over 2.5 million Facebook posts. The coverage highlights how Meta's political content suppression was highly asymmetrical—disproportionately penalizing institutional politicians while failing to restrict extremist and Eurosceptic ecosystems.
description: An investigative news article by Open covering a study led by Fabio Giglietto on how Facebook's algorithm disproportionately penalizes mainstream political content while failing to restrict extremist ecosystems.
